name: Monorepo with npm Workspaces
description: Set up and manage a monorepo using npm workspaces to share code, dependencies, and scripts across multiple packages in a single repository.
trendingReason: Monorepos are becoming standard for managing multi-package projects without external tools

steps.title: Initialize the root package
steps.description: Create the root package.json with workspace definitions.
steps.command: npm init -y && npm pkg set workspaces='["packages/*"]' private=true
steps.tip: The root package must have 'private: true' to prevent accidentally publishing the monorepo root.

steps.title: Create workspace packages
steps.description: Scaffold individual packages within the workspaces directory.
steps.command: mkdir -p packages/shared packages/api packages/web && npm init -y -w packages/shared && npm init -y -w packages/api && npm init -y -w packages/web

steps.title: Add a dependency to a specific workspace
steps.description: Install a package into one workspace without affecting others.
steps.command: npm install express -w packages/api && npm install react react-dom -w packages/web

steps.title: Link a workspace as a dependency
steps.description: Reference one workspace package from another for code sharing.
steps.command: npm install packages/shared -w packages/api && npm install packages/shared -w packages/web
steps.tip: npm automatically creates a symlink. The shared package will appear in the workspace's package.json as a regular dependency.

steps.title: Run scripts across workspaces
steps.description: Execute npm scripts in specific or all workspaces.
steps.command: npm run build --workspaces && npm run test -w packages/api

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# npm Workspaces Monorepo Setup
set -euo pipefail

PROJECT=${1:?"Usage: $0 <project-name>"}

echo "=== Creating Monorepo: $PROJECT ==="
mkdir -p "$PROJECT" && cd "$PROJECT"

# Root package.json
npm init -y
npm pkg set private=true
npm pkg set workspaces='["packages/*"]'

# Create workspace packages
for pkg in shared api web; do
  mkdir -p "packages/$pkg/src"
  cd "packages/$pkg"
  npm init -y
  npm pkg set name="@$PROJECT/$pkg"
  echo "export {};" > src/index.ts
  cd ../..
done

# Link shared package to api and web
npm install "./packages/shared" -w "packages/api"
npm install "./packages/shared" -w "packages/web"

echo ""
echo "=== Workspace Structure ==="
find . -name 'package.json' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' | sort

echo ""
echo "=== Workspace Info ==="
npm ls --workspaces --depth=0

echo ""
echo "Monorepo created at $PROJECT/"

faq.question: How do npm workspaces differ from Lerna?
faq.answer: npm workspaces handle dependency hoisting and cross-package linking natively. Lerna adds versioning, changelog generation, and selective publishing on top. Many projects now use npm workspaces alone or combine them with Changesets for versioning.

faq.question: How do I run a script in only one workspace?
faq.answer: Use the -w flag: 'npm run build -w packages/api'. You can also use --workspace with the package name: 'npm run build --workspace=@myorg/api'.

faq.question: Can I hoist a dependency to the root?
faq.answer: npm 7+ automatically hoists shared dependencies to the root node_modules. If a workspace needs a different version, npm installs that version locally in the workspace's own node_modules.
